segments.text: Welcome to your daily dose of robotics news for July 1, 2026.
Today, we're covering everything from soft robotic hardware integration and major ROS advancements, to developer speed tips.
Grab your coffee; it’s time for the update!
From The Robot Report, "Soft, robotic cells from morph embed physical AI into hardware."
This development details a new platform enabling the integration of artificial intelligence directly within physical robotic components.
Morph has unveiled its novel soft robotics technology, allowing for the design and manufacture of units termed "soft robotic cells."

These specialized cells represent an advancement in physically intelligent systems, indicating a method to build computation directly into the hardware structure itself rather than relying solely on external processing units.
The initial announcement confirms this capability as a significant step toward more embodied artificial intelligence.
This development suggests a future trajectory for robotics focusing on highly integrated and physically adaptive machine intelligence.
From Planet ROS, "ROS Industrial: ros2_canopen: Natively Integrating CANopen Devices into the ROS 2 Ecosystem."
This report details a method allowing seamless incorporation of CANopen devices directly within the ROS 2 framework.

The integration is achieved through the utilization of lely-core for handling low-level protocol requirements, enabling users to define an entire CAN bus setup via one YAML description that subsequently launches both master and device drivers.

Developers can execute operations using standard nodes or lifecycle nodes, alongside full compatibility with ros2_control for advanced control implementations, featuring a generic Proxy Driver and a dedicated CiA 402 motion control driver, notably supporting multi-drive coordination within single controlling nodes.
This advancement streamlines hardware integration, providing clearer pathways for robotics systems utilizing industry-standard industrial communication protocols.
From NVIDIA Blog, "How Jaiveer Singh Is Helping Robots — and Developers — Move Faster."
This report details foundational work advancing robotics through essential infrastructure development rather than showcasing only final demonstrations.
The focus involves developing core components like internal circuit boards and supporting software necessary for advanced robotic function.

Specifically, the described efforts include creating tools that facilitate developers' processing of data streams originating from robot cameras, alongside addressing complex engineering required for operation outside controlled test settings.
This body of work directly expedites the practical deployment potential across the robotics technology sector.
The advancements point toward a measurable acceleration in the real-world applicability and widespread integration of sophisticated robotic systems.
From The Robot Report, "X Square Robot brings its valuation to $2.8B with four consecutive funding rounds."
This report details the recent financial achievements and technological standing of X Square Robotics.

The company operates a comprehensive system that merges foundation models, specialized robotics hardware, and a robust data pipeline, all components engineered for successful real-world deployment across diverse applications.
Following this sequence of four consecutive financing rounds, X Square Robotics has officially reached an enterprise valuation of $2.8 billion.
This substantial funding achievement underscores growing investor confidence in the integration of advanced AI frameworks with physical robotic systems within the industrial sector.
From The Robot Report, "Queue raises funding to build fully autonomous pharmacy."
This story details the advancement of automated pharmacy units designed to enhance prescription accessibility across diverse community settings.
These advanced systems are engineered for complete autonomy in operation.
Deployment plans encompass a variety of locations including hospital facilities, retail store environments, and medically underserved regions.
Recent reports confirm that the company has successfully secured the requisite funding necessary to move forward with this significant expansion.
The core development objective remains increasing public reach for routine medication access through automated means.
This represents a notable step in applying autonomous robotics toward improving localized healthcare infrastructure.
From MIT News - Robotics, "MIT student teams win top honors in NASA competition."
The report details three MIT teams achieving significant success at the 2026 NASA RASC-AL Competition, earning a total of five top awards during the event.

The competition's focus involved developing essential components designated for future lunar bases, while the participating groups also addressed complex designs requisite for forthcoming missions targeted toward Mars.
These achievements underscore the depth of student ingenuity within the field of aerospace engineering.
The demonstrated capabilities suggest accelerated progress in developing autonomous systems crucial for deep-space exploration initiatives across various technological sectors.
From Robotics & Automation News, "How to build a WordPress website for an industrial business."
The report details how a single online platform can manage several critical functions for businesses operating in the industrial sector.
These essential functionalities include establishing mechanisms for capturing potential leads and generating new customer interest.
Furthermore, such a site must effectively showcase the company's established industry expertise and provide a centralized system for organizing customer service activities.
This focus underscores the necessity of robust digital infrastructure within traditional industrial operations moving forward.
That’s all for your July 1st deep dive into the bot world!
Remember those soft robots?
The future is truly yielding.
Thanks for tuning in, and keep building amazing things!
Bye for now.
